title: Spring Fresh Egyptian Lamb Kebabs
description: A flavorful and festive Egyptian dish featuring lamb, canola oil, and spring fresh ingredients, perfect for a sugar-free friendly diet. This earthy and springy recipe is sure to delight your taste buds.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together canola oil, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the lamb cubes to the marinade and toss to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill to medium-high heat and thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until the lamb is cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees.
Let the kebabs rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
Serve with a side of quinoa or rice and your favorite vegetables.
Leftover kebabs can be refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 2 months.
